§6Constructing Machines: The Robot Assembler§f

§aAssemble advanced mobile units, drones, and microcontrollers to perform mechanical tasks!§f
Mobile entities like Robots and Drones cannot be crafted in a normal crafting grid. Instead, you must build a specialized §bRobot Assembler§f to program and piece their hardware together.

§6Assembler Mechanics§f:
§b• Energy Consumption§f: The Robot Assembler draws §e50 energy per tick§f while active, which directly controls the speed of the assembly process.
§b• Base Templates§f: You must place a template item in the assembler's main slot (e.g., a §bComputer Case§f for a robot, or a §bDrone Case§f for a drone) and then populate the expansion slots.

§6Assembly Complexity & Energy Costs§f:
Every component you add to a machine increases its complexity. High complexity requires more time and a larger cumulative energy input to complete assembly:
§b• Robots§f: 
  §e• Base Energy Cost§f: §e50,000 energy§f.
  §e• Complexity Cost§f: §e+10,000 energy§f per point of hardware complexity.
§b• Drones§f: 
  §e• Base Energy Cost§f: §e25,000 energy§f.
  §e• Complexity Cost§f: §e+15,000 energy§f per point of hardware complexity.
§b• Microcontrollers (MCUs)§f: 
  §e• Base Energy Cost§f: §e10,000 energy§f.
  §e• Complexity Cost§f: §e+10,000 energy§f per point of hardware complexity.

§9How to Assemble§f:
1. Provide power to the §bRobot Assembler§f.
2. Place a Case (e.g., Robot Case) in the top slot.
3. Insert components (CPU, RAM, Screen, Keyboard, Cards, and Upgrade modules like Inventory Controllers).
4. Click the §9Assemble§f button in the GUI. The progress bar will fill as it consumes power. Once finished, retrieve your newly assembled mobile device!
